name: Consumer Bankruptcy Assistance Project
description_short: Non-profit bankruptcy law firm providing free Chapter 7 filing assistance to low-income Philadelphia residents through trained volunteer attorneys since 1992.
description_long:
Consumer Bankruptcy Assistance Project (CBAP) is a Philadelphia-based non-profit organization that has provided free legal services for over 25 years, with a mission to help low-income families achieve financial stability through bankruptcy relief. Founded in 1992, CBAP operates from their office at 718 Arch Street in Philadelphia and serves clients who face overwhelming debt from catastrophic life events such as serious illness or sudden job loss.

CBAP offers free Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing services to financially eligible clients, paired with trained volunteer attorneys who guide them through the entire process from initial consultation to court appearance. They also provide financial counseling and debt management assistance to help clients determine whether bankruptcy is the appropriate solution or if alternative debt management strategies would be more beneficial. Additionally, the organization conducts Credit Abuse Resistance Education seminars for young adults and community groups on topics including credit fundamentals, budgeting, and student loan management.

What distinguishes CBAP is their volunteer-attorney model combined with zero cost to eligible clients, their 25+ year track record specifically serving low-income Philadelphia residents, and their emphasis on financial education alongside legal services. All volunteer attorneys receive specialized training in Chapter 7 bankruptcy procedures, ensuring consistent expertise. The organization takes a comprehensive team approach, providing ongoing support throughout the filing process rather than transactional document preparation.

CBAP is an excellent resource for qualifying low-income Philadelphia residents seeking legitimate bankruptcy relief, though potential clients must meet financial eligibility requirements and should note that services are limited to Chapter 7 filings (not Chapter 13 reorganizations). The organization's reliance on volunteers means scheduling may have longer wait times compared to private firms, and intake calls are limited to specific hours (Monday-Thursday, 9:30am-12:00pm).
pros: Completely free legal services for financially eligible clients—no hidden fees or payment plans required
All volunteer attorneys receive specialized Chapter 7 bankruptcy training, ensuring expertise in this specific practice area
25+ year track record serving low-income Philadelphia families with established credibility and organizational stability
Team-based approach means assigned volunteer stays with client from intake through court appearance, not just document preparation
Integrated financial counseling helps clients determine if bankruptcy is appropriate before filing, avoiding unnecessary legal proceedings
Offers supplemental Credit Abuse Resistance Education to community groups and young adults on credit management and budgeting
Toll-free phone number (215-523-9511) and online intake option provide accessible entry points for prospective clients
cons: Financial eligibility requirements may exclude some middle-income households seeking bankruptcy assistance
Limited to Chapter 7 bankruptcy only—does not offer Chapter 13 reorganization filings for those needing debt repayment plans
Intake intake phone line operates only Monday-Thursday, 9:30am-12:00pm, which may not accommodate all working-schedule constraints
Volunteer-based model may result in longer wait times and less predictable scheduling compared to private law firms
Service area appears limited to Philadelphia residents based on office location and website messaging
best_for: Low-income Philadelphia residents facing overwhelming debt from medical emergencies, job loss, or other catastrophic life events
Families seeking Chapter 7 bankruptcy discharge who qualify financially and want guidance from experienced attorneys at no cost
Individuals wanting to understand their full financial options through counseling before committing to bankruptcy filing
Community organizations and young adults seeking financial education on credit, budgeting, and debt management
diagnosis: CBAP is ideal for low-income Philadelphia residents who qualify financially and need experienced Chapter 7 bankruptcy representation at no cost, particularly those whose debt stems from life emergencies. The primary caveat is that services are limited to Chapter 7 filings, financial eligibility is required, and intake availability is restricted to specific weekday hours, potentially creating delays for some applicants.
services: Free Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and legal representation for eligible clients
Initial financial situation review and debt assessment
Financial counseling and debt management guidance
Determination of bankruptcy eligibility and suitability
Credit Abuse Resistance Education presentations for community groups
Community agency staff training on financial topics

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 718 Arch St, Philadelphia, PA 19106, USA
